sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen are in excellent form, winning 4 of their last 5 matches, including a dominant 6-0 victory over Heidenheim and a 2-0 win against Manchester City. Borussia Dortmund have been solid but less consistent, with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss in their last 5, highlighted by a 4-0 thrashing of Villarreal but a recent 3-3 draw against Stuttgart. Leverkusen's attacking prowess and recent momentum give them a slight edge at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, matches have been high-scoring with both teams finding the net. Dortmund won the most recent clash 4-2 in May 2025, while Leverkusen edged a 3-2 thriller in January 2025. Historical trends suggest goals on both sides, with neither team keeping a clean sheet in their last 17 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Leverkusen, Alejandro Grimaldo (7.51 average rating) and Patrik Schick (4 goals in last 5 matches) are in top form, posing a significant threat. Dortmund will rely on Serhou Guirassy (5 goals this season) and Karim Adeyemi for attacking output, though the absence of Maximilian Beier might impact their depth. Leverkusen's in-form players could make the difference.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen rank 2nd in Bundesliga for goals scored (27) and big chances created (42), showcasing their offensive strength. Dortmund, with 19 goals, have a tighter defense (10 conceded vs Leverkusen’s 15), but their recent inconsistency might be exploited by Leverkusen’s high possession (58.6%) and accurate passing (88.9%). The home side’s stats lean towards an attacking edge.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Leverkusen’s ability to dominate possession and create fast breaks (1.9 per game, 2nd in league) could test Dortmund’s defense, especially in the final third. Dortmund often strike late (50% of goals after 76 minutes) and will look to exploit counter-attacks, but Leverkusen’s ground duel success (53.1%) might neutralize this. Expect Leverkusen to control the tempo early.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Despite Sofascore’s prediction favoring Dortmund (49% away win vs 24% home win), Leverkusen’s recent form, home advantage at BayArena, and superior attacking stats tilt the balance slightly in their favor. However, Dortmund’s resilience and history in this fixture suggest a tight contest. A high-scoring draw or narrow Leverkusen win seems plausible.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Borussia Dortmund lead Bayer Leverkusen 1-0 at the BayArena. The away side took the lead through a headed goal by Aarón Anselmino in the 41st minute. Leverkusen, despite dominating possession and creating more chances, failed to convert their opportunities. Dortmund capitalized on a late first-half moment to secure their advantage. Both teams have shown intent, but Leverkusen will feel they need to be more clinical in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Leverkusen controlled the ball for the majority of the first half and managed to create a big chance, though they missed it. They entered the penalty area frequently and earned several corners, indicating a strong attacking presence. However, their inability to win duels in the air and lack of possession wins in the final third hindered their effectiveness. Dortmund, on the other hand, were more efficient despite less possession. They entered the final third and penalty area often, won more aerial duels, and secured possessions in dangerous areas, which ultimately led to their goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Leverkusen, Alejandro Grimaldo stood out with key passes that created opportunities, while Ibrahim Maza contributed with a big chance created. Ernest Poku showed promise with a shot on target but missed a big chance. On Dortmund’s side, Aarón Anselmino was the standout performer, scoring the crucial goal and maintaining a strong defensive presence with clearances. Daniel Svensson also played a pivotal role with an assist and key passes that helped build attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Initially, Dortmund dominated with consistent attacking spells, pushing forward with intent in the early stages. Leverkusen responded with a strong period of pressure around the midway point of the half, showing dangerous intent in attack. However, their momentum dipped, and Dortmund regained control, culminating in their goal just before the break. Towards the end of the half, Leverkusen tried to push back, but they couldn’t sustain the intensity to level the score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Leverkusen are expected to come out aggressively in the second half, looking to capitalize on their possession advantage and convert their chances. They will likely focus on creating more big opportunities through players like Grimaldo and Poku. Dortmund, with a lead to protect, may adopt a more defensive approach while seeking counter-attacking opportunities through players like Serhou Guirassy. Discipline will be key for both sides, as yellow cards have already been shown, and Leverkusen’s frustration could lead to further bookings if they fail to break through.
